commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix zero gradients in FWI surrogate: replace zero_module with small init

Use small normal init (std=0.01) instead so gradients propagate from step 1.

commit.message:
Fix CR/newline in station names & redesign event row layout

[Bug Fix]
- KTXParser.cleanStation: filter { !$0.isWhitespace } 로 변경
  → \s character class가 \r·\n을 역명에 캡처하는 문제 근본 차단
  → trimmingCharacters는 양끝만 제거하므로 중간 삽입 방어 불가했음
- ContentView.titleParts: EventKit 제목 읽을 때 \r\n·\r·\n 제거
  → 구버전 앱이 저장한 이벤트에 잔존하는 \r도 방어

[Layout Redesign]
- 1행: 출발역 → 도착역(좌) + HH:mm → HH:mm(우)
- 2행: 열차 편명 (보조)
- 시간 HStack에 .fixedSize() 적용 → 편명 길이와 무관하게 줄바꿈 원천 차단
- 날짜 뱃지: M.d + 요일(E) + 예정/완료 분리 표시
- 시간 폰트: .monospaced 적용으로 자릿수 흔들림 없음

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add MoveMessage and Rescan methods to gRPC client (#16)

The Move and Rescan RPCs exist in the proto definitions and are
implemented server-side but were missing from the client library.

MoveMessage enables atomic cross-folder moves (needed by imapd's
MOVE command). Rescan returns only new messages since the last
List/Rescan, used by IMAP IDLE to detect new mail.

https://github.com/infodancer/mail-session/issues/15

commit.message:
Schedule strength on hard days, not easy days

Follow the "Hard Days Hard, Easy Days Easy" principle:
- Pair strength with Interval/Tempo runs on the same day (not standalone days)
- Always run first, then lift
- Target 2-3 sessions x 30min per week, taper to 1 in taper weeks
- Keep workout_type as the run type so hard-day spacing detection is unaffected;
  strength session described in description/notes fields

- GET /api/jobs/[id] now returns Cache-Control: no-store so Vercel
  edge never caches the response
- Clicking the Ranked tab always triggers a fresh load() so scores
  are visible immediately after a Score All run

Add auth middleware for protected endpoint enforcement (Phase A1.4)

Auth middleware validates access_token cookie and sets request.state.user
on every request. Protected paths (/api/cases, /api/discovery,
/api/objections) return 401 without valid token. Public paths (RAG Q&A,
calculators, intake, feedback) pass through. Rate limiter upgraded to use
user_id for authenticated users instead of IP. 39 new tests.

for sovereign). Content modeled on post-quantum page structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Made-with: Cursor

Fix speaker permutation alignment across sliding windows

Each 10s window independently assigns speaker IDs, so the same speaker
could be spk1 in window A but spk2 in window B. Without alignment,
averaging probabilities makes single-speaker regions look like overlap.

Now aligns speaker order between consecutive windows using MSE-based
permutation matching on the overlapping region before averaging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix notification click causing UI misalignment across all tabs (#101)

The service worker was caching full server-rendered HTML for all navigation
requests and falling back to cached '/' (Feed page) on network failure.
On spotty mobile networks (common when waking phone to tap a notification),
this caused Feed page content to bleed into every tab.

- Remove navigation response caching from SW fetch handler (Next.js pages
  are dynamic and must always come from the network)
- Bump SW cache version to v5 to purge stale cached HTML
- Harden notificationclick handler with try/catch + async/await to handle
  frozen/discarded mobile tabs, falling back to openWindow
- Add ScrollRestorer component to reset scroll position on route changes

commit.message:
Fix CI: read INTERNAL_SECRET at call time, not module load time

The secret was captured in a module-level const, so in CI the module
could be cached before test setup sets process.env.INTERNAL_SECRET,
causing all authenticated requests to return 401.

call that properly handles NextAuth's CSRF flow. Use absolute callback URL
with window.location.origin to ensure it works across all environments.

commit.message:
Fix black stream: go2rtc RTSP on 0.0.0.0 + real host IP in stream URLs

go2rtc was listening on 127.0.0.1:8554 (loopback only), meaning
UniFi Protect on the network could never reach it. Also, the RTSP
URL given to the ONVIF server was rtsp://127.0.0.1:8554/... — from
Protect's perspective on a different machine, 127.0.0.1 points to
the UDM itself, not the HA host. Fixed both: RTSP now binds to
0.0.0.0:8554 and the host's real LAN IP (e.g. 192.168.1.251) is
used in the stream URI reported to Protect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add @tailwindcss/vite plugin to enable Tailwind CSS utility generation

Tailwind CSS 4.0 requires either @tailwindcss/vite or @tailwindcss/postcss
plugin to process utility classes. Without it, the built CSS contained only
base/reset styles with an empty @layer utilities block, causing the layout
to be completely broken on GitHub Pages.
